name: Shetland Islands (Lerwick)
summary: Shetland is a windswept group of more than 100 Scottish islands far up in the North Sea, closer to Norway than to London. It is famous for tiny Shetland ponies, Viking history, and clifftops packed with puffins.
funFacts: Vikings ruled Shetland for 500 years, so kids can spot Norse names on almost every village, hill and bay across the islands.
Shetland ponies are the smallest horse breed in the world, only about a metre tall, but strong enough to once haul coal from deep mines.
Every January around 1,000 people in Lerwick dress as Vikings, march with flaming torches, and burn a giant wooden longship called a galley.
Puffins, the orange-beaked 'clowns of the sea', return to Sumburgh Head's clifftop burrows every April after eight whole months out at sea.
In midsummer the sun barely sets at all. Locals call this glowing twilight the 'simmer dim' and read books outside at midnight.
thingsToDo: Wander Lerwick's old stone-paved Commercial Street and peek at the Lodberrie waterfront houses jutting straight into the sea.
Climb around Mousa Broch, a 13-metre Iron Age stone tower on a tiny island, the tallest broch still standing in the world.
Visit Sumburgh Head lighthouse and watch thousands of puffins nesting in burrows along the grassy clifftop path.
Explore Jarlshof's incredible jumble of Stone Age, Iron Age, Viking and medieval ruins poking out of the grass by the sea.
Walk across the sandy tombolo bridge to St Ninian's Isle, one of Europe's largest sand bars connecting an island to land.
Meet real Shetland ponies on a 'puffins and ponies' minibus tour, or spot them grazing in fields across the windswept Mainland.

wildlife.name: Shetland Pony
wildlife.description: Tiny shaggy horse only a metre tall, with a thick mane built for icy island winters.

wildlife.name: Atlantic Puffin
wildlife.description: Black and white seabird with a bright orange beak, nesting in clifftop burrows at Sumburgh Head.

wildlife.name: Eurasian Otter
wildlife.description: Playful brown swimmer that hunts crabs and fish along Shetland's quiet rocky shores.

wildlife.name: Killer Whale (Orca)
wildlife.description: Huge black and white dolphin often spotted hunting seals from Shetland's clifftop paths.

localWord.meaning: Small or little in the Shetland dialect — locals say 'peerie bairn' for a small child.

images.alt: Old stone houses lining Lerwick's narrow flagstone-paved Commercial Street.
Ancient stone ruins of Jarlshof, a 4,000-year-old prehistoric and Norse settlement on the southern tip of Shetland.
The tall round Iron Age stone tower of Mousa Broch standing on the island of Mousa.
The white Sumburgh Head lighthouse perched on top of a steep grassy sea cliff.
A long curving sandy tombolo bridge linking St Ninian's Isle to the Shetland Mainland.
A fluffy little Shetland pony, less than four feet tall, looking right at the camera.
Colourful Atlantic puffins perched on the grassy clifftops of Sumburgh Head in Shetland.
A wet Eurasian otter sitting on the rocks at Toft pier in Shetland.
The tall black dorsal fin of a killer whale slicing above the waves.
A Viking longship burning in flames during the Up Helly Aa fire festival in Shetland.
